m_Item1.QuestionText: Which out of the following is not the advantage of AJAX?

Options.OptionText: Ajax reduces server traffic and increase speed

Ajax allows its users to make asynchronous calls to the web server without reloading the whole web page. As a web visitor, you don’t have to wait for the entire page to load entirely in order to access the entire page content.
Ajax reduces bandwidth usage which is effective in improving web performance
Ajax pages can not be indexed by Google as well as other search engines

m_Item1.Explanation: Search Engines cannot index Ajax pages is the major disadvanatge of ajax

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jquery function allows to set function to specify what to do in case of success or failure of the request respectively while making asynchronous request?
m_Item1.Explanation: $.ajax() function accepts an object in which we can set various options for making asynchronous request

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function is called every time when XMLHttpRequest (XHR) readyState changes?
m_Item1.Explanation: The readystatechange event is triggered every time the readyState property of an XMLHttpRequest object changes, indicating that there has been a change in the state of the request. Function is assigned for onreadystatechange property which gets executed for every ready state change

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which property in AJAX is used for reading data from the server-side?
m_Item1.Explanation: The responseText property in AJAX is used to retrieve the response from the server as a string. It contains the response from the server as text, allowing you to read the data sent by the server in the response. Options A, C, and D represent other properties and methods in AJAX but are not specifically used for reading data from the server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which function in jQuery is commonly used for sending a GET request in AJAX?
m_Item1.Explanation: $.get(url, callback) is commonly used or sending a GET request in AJAX .

m_Item1.QuestionText: AJAX is combination of
m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX uses javascript syntax to make request asynchronously. Using HTML DOM page gets modified after receiving response using XMLHttpRequest object.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Ajax request pass through total how many readystate
m_Item1.Explanation: readyState of AJAX request object pass through 5 different states - 0,1,2,3,4

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which method of Ajax hit server
m_Item1.Explanation: send() method of ajax hit server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: _________ jQuery method is used to perform an asynchronous HTTP request?
m_Item1.Explanation: ajax() is a jquery method used for making asynchronous request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: AJAX allows web page to dynamically
m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX allows web page to dynamically change content of web page(loaded from server).

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which property is used to check whether AJAX request has been completed
m_Item1.Explanation: readyState property is used to check whether AJAX request has completed or not.

m_Item1.QuestionText: The jQuery AJAX methods .get(), .post(), and .ajax() all require which parameter to be supplied?
m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX methods get,post and ajax all require url as a parameter to be supplied.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Load remote data using HTTP GET
m_Item1.Explanation: Load Remote data using $.get(url,callback)  method.

m_Item1.QuestionText: ______________ is the sever support AJAX ?
m_Item1.Explanation: HTTP is the sever support AJAX

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following are the features of an HTTP request?
m_Item1.Explanation:
An HTTP request consists of four parts :
the HTTP request method or “verb”
the URL being requested
an optional set of request headers, which may include authentication information
an optional request body

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is a feature of the HTTP response?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The HTTP response sent by a server has three parts :
a numeric and textual status code that indicates the success or failure of the request
a set of response headers
the response body

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which is the appropriate code to initialize a HTTP GET request asynchronouly?
m_Item1.Explanation:
The code that begins a HTTP GET request for the contents of the specified URL is
request.open("GET","data.csv");
To send a request to a server open() and send() methods of the XMLHttpRequest object are used.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which one of the technologies is NOT used in AJAX?
m_Item1.Explanation: Flash technology is not used in AJAX.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What was the name for Microsoft’s version of AJAX?
m_Item1.Explanation: Microsoft's version of AJAX was called Atlas.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Using AJAX we can make our web page ___________

Options.OptionText: easy to connect web page with server

m_Item1.Explanation: Using AJAX we can make our web page more interactive and faster.

m_Item1.QuestionText: AJAX Stands for:
m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX long form is Asynchronous Javascript and XML.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does the XMLHttpRequest object accomplish in Ajax?

Options.OptionText: It's the programming language used to develop Ajax applications.
It provide the ability to exchange structured data between client and server synchronously.
It provides the ability to asynchronously exchange data between Web browsers and a Web server.
It provides the ability to mark up and style the display of Web-page text.

m_Item1.Explanation: XMLHttpRequest object  provides the ability to asynchronously exchange data between Web browsers and a Web server.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Users can not use ajax when

Options.OptionText: a page need to be shown in a search engine
user wants to create secure applications

m_Item1.Explanation: User can't use ajax when browser does not support javascript, wants to create secure applications(chance of web security vulnerability if not implented properly) and a page need to be shown in a search engine.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does AJAX stand for in web programming?
m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX stands for Asynchronous JavaScript and XML. It allows web pages to be updated asynchronously by exchanging data with a web server behind the scenes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following best describes the main advantage of using AJAX in web development?

Options.OptionText: AJAX allows for synchronous data loading.
AJAX enables dynamic data updates without refreshing the entire page.

m_Item1.Explanation: The main advantage of AJAX is that it allows web pages to be updated asynchronously, which means parts of the page can be updated without having to reload the entire page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is commonly used with AJAX to request data from a server?
m_Item1.Explanation: The GET method is commonly used in AJAX requests to retrieve data from a server, as it appends data to the URL and is used to request data without causing a change in the server’s state.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In an AJAX request, which of the following is used to handle the response data?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'onreadystatechange' event is used in AJAX to handle the response data. It is triggered every time the readyState changes, and it is within this event where you typically define the actions to be taken with the response data once it has been completely received.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What data format is commonly associated with AJAX for asynchronous data exchange besides XML?
m_Item1.Explanation: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is commonly used with AJAX for asynchronous data exchange due to its lightweight nature and easy integration with JavaScript.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What is the primary purpose of using an AJAX framework in web development?

Options.OptionText: To improve routing efficiency on the client-side.

m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX frameworks are primarily used to enable asynchronous data exchanges between the client and server without the need to reload the entire web page. This enhances the user experience by making web applications faster and more dynamic.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which component of the AJAX architecture is responsible for handling requests and responses to and from the server?
m_Item1.Explanation: The XMLHttpRequest object is a crucial component in the AJAX architecture, utilized for sending HTTP requests to the server and processing server responses asynchronously.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the AJAX model, which technique is commonly used to update part of a web page without refreshing the entire page?
m_Item1.Explanation: DOM manipulation allows developers to update sections of a web page dynamically in response to AJAX events, without the necessity to reload the whole page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which of the following is not a typical feature provided by AJAX frameworks?
m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX frameworks are designed to facilitate asynchronous (not synchronous) data processing to enhance user interactivity and web application responsiveness.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How do AJAX frameworks generally improve the loading speed of web pages?

Options.OptionText: By only updating the parts of the page that need changes.
By reducing the number of images used on the site.

m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX allows for partial page updates, meaning only the necessary parts of the page are reloaded or updated, making it faster and more efficient, as opposed to reloading the entire web page.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does AJAX stand for in web development?
m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX stands for Asynchronous JavaScript and XML, a set of web development techniques that enable web pages to be updated asynchronously by exchanging small amounts of data with the server behind the scenes.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which JavaScript object is used to send AJAX requests in modern browsers?
m_Item1.Explanation: The XMLHttpRequest object is used to exchange data asynchronously between a client and a server. It is a core component of Ajax.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What method is commonly used to initialize an AJAX request?
m_Item1.Explanation: The .open() method is used to initialize an AJAX request by specifying the HTTP method, the target URL, and other optional parameters.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which HTTP method is commonly used to send data in an AJAX request?
m_Item1.Explanation: The POST method is commonly used in AJAX requests to send data to the server, especially when submitting form data or sending large amounts of data.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What property should you check to determine if an AJAX request was completed successfully?
m_Item1.Explanation: The status property of an XMLHttpRequest object should be checked to determine if an AJAX request was completed successfully. A status of 200 indicates success.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What does AJAX stand for in the context of web development?
m_Item1.Explanation: AJAX stands for Asynchronous JavaScript and XML. It is a technique used in web development to create fast and dynamic web pages.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which jQuery method is commonly used to perform asynchronous HTTP requests?
m_Item1.Explanation: $.ajax() is the most flexible and commonly used method to perform asynchronous HTTP requests in jQuery. It offers extensive options for configuration.

m_Item1.QuestionText: What type of data can jQuery's AJAX methods send and receive?

Options.OptionText: JSON, HTML, XML, and Plain Text

m_Item1.Explanation: jQuery's AJAX methods can handle multiple data types including JSON, HTML, XML, and Plain Text, which are specified by the 'dataType' option.

m_Item1.QuestionText: In the $.ajax() method, which option is used to specify the HTTP method to use for the request?
m_Item1.Explanation: The 'type' option in the $.ajax() method specifies the HTTP method (e.g., 'GET', 'POST') to be used for the request.

m_Item1.QuestionText: How can you execute a function when an AJAX request completes successfully using jQuery?

Options.OptionText: Set a global AJAX event handler
Provide a success callback function in $.ajax()

m_Item1.Explanation: By providing a success callback function in the $.ajax() options, you can execute code when an AJAX request completes successfully.

m_Item1.QuestionText: Which readyState value means the request is complete and the response is fully received?
m_Item1.Explanation: A readyState of 4 means the operation is complete and the response is ready to be processed.
